item.title: Poxvirus F13L homodimer-interface targeted by BoltzGen cyclic-minipeptides and permeable mutants
item.description: Hundreds of Boltzgen cyclic-minipeptide-conformer candidates of different sizes targeted the vaccinia poxvirus F13L homodimer-interface (F13Lmin) in a Tecovirimat-binding manner. However, only low affinity candidates < 6-mer predicted membrane-...

item.title: From Hydrothermal Vents to Ocean Worlds: the Prebiotic Chemistry of Ketene
item.description: Living systems are sustained far from equilibrium by continuous, enzyme-mediated turnover of chemical fuels. How comparable chemistry could begin without evolved catalysts, and against the thermodynamic cost of assembling complex molecules in water, ...

item.title: Data-Driven Multi-Objective Analysis of Activity–Poisoning resistance Trade-Offs in Complex Multimetallic Electrocatalysts for Glucose Oxidation
item.description: The discovery of efficient and durable multimetallic nanoparticle electrocatalysts requires effective navigation of vast compositional spaces. However, systematically exploring these spaces while balancing competing performance objectives remains ...

item.title: Association-gated multiphoton activation of inert haloarenes via sequential electron donor–acceptor assembly
item.description: The functionalization of recalcitrant chemical bonds that require extreme reduction potentials is often thwarted by the absence of a defined reaction field to control potent intermediates. Conventional multiphoton photocatalysis generates super-reductants,...

item.title: Reconfigurable Peptide Nanostructures via Sequence and Solvent Tuning
item.description: Peptide self-assembly in aqueous media is dominated by hydrophobic collapse and backbone hydrogen bonding, further stabilized by side-chain-specific interactions such as cation-π, aromatic stacking, and electrostatics. However, under non-aqueous and low-...

item.title: BeamStop: A software for analysing chemical imaging and tomography data
item.description: Chemical imaging datasets, particularly those from techniques like X-ray powder diffraction imaging and tomography (XRD-CT), are challenging to analyse due to their high dimensionality, complexity, and spatial heterogeneity. BeamStop is a specialised ...

item.title: Dynamic solid-liquid equilibrium controls propylene electrochemical epoxidation on silver-based catalysts
item.description: Although silver-based catalysts are the benchmark for thermocatalytic epoxidation of propylene, they are considered as subpar compared to Pd-based catalysts for the electrocatalytic epoxidation of propylene. Recent reports have challenged this view, ...

item.title: Iterative Constructing and Screening of DNA-Encoded Libraries Enable the Discovery and Structure Optimization of Macrocyclic Hit Compounds
item.description: Macrocyclic peptides occupy a privileged "middle space" in drug discovery, offering the specificity of biologics combined with the synthetic tractability of small molecules. However, the rapid identification and structural optimization of macrocyclic ...

item.title: Soft Nano/Microgels Observed in situ at Fluid Interfaces:Recent Progress and Future Perspectives
item.description: In this invited focus review, recent progress in understanding the interfacial behaviors of soft nano/micro hydrogels (hereafter referred to as microgels) is reviewed, with particular emphasis on research done by the authors’ group. Unlike rigid polymeric ...

item.title: Late-Stage C4-Selective Pyridine Alkylation Enabled by Benzhydryl Activation

item.title: Features of the Gibbs Equation (∆G = ∆H - T∆S) Applied to Solvation, Vaporization, and Complexation Thermodynamics
item.description: The Gibbs equation (∆G= ∆H–T∆S) remains a cornerstone of chemical thermodynamics. Yet the scope and origins of the widely observed correlations between the enthalpies (∆H) and entropies (∆S), or Gibbs energies (∆G) and enthalpies, have long been ...

item.title: High-speed photoresponse of CVD-grown lateral TMD heterostructures
item.description: Although chemical vapor deposition (CVD) enables wafer-scale fabrication of transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD) photodetectors, CVD-grown devices are often limited by high defect densities and are generally considered significantly slower than devices ...

item.title: pH-Dependent Free Energies of Insulin Fibril Assembly from Protonation Ensemble Statistics
item.description: Protonation equilibria govern the pH-dependent stability of protein aggregates, yet their thermodynamic implications remain difficult to quantify. Here, we establish a direct connection between site-unresolved protonation statistics and pH-dependent free ...

item.title: Improved Aromatic Finkelstein Reactions for the Synthesis of Iodinated BODIPYs
item.description: Iodinated 4,4-difluoro-4-bora-3a,4a-diaza-s-indacenes (BODIPYs) are valuable synthetic intermediates for the formation of functionalised fluorophores, as they enable downstream nucleophilic aromatic substitution (SNAr) and metal-catalysed cross-coupling ...

item.title: Investigating the Stacking Diversity in 2D COFs: Implications for Structural and Elastic Properties
item.description: 2D covalent organic frameworks (COFs) are layered organic materials that often adopt multiple stacking configurations or exhibit disordered stacking motifs. The stacking type is known to have a significant influence on their properties. However, only a ...

item.description: The amino acid-to-enzyme, part-to-whole transition leads to the emergence of enzymatic complexity and diversity. The conventional convergent, heuristic enzymology approach typically classifies enzymes into defined catalytic categories for the predictive ...

item.title: Persistent Homology using Nets: Topologically Informed Machine Learning Descriptors of Framework Materials
item.description: Topological nets define the connectivity in framework materials, influencing their pore structure and mechanical properties. In this work, we investigate the importance of incorporating this information into machine learning models for gas adsorption ...

item.title: Operating Conditions Redefine MOF Design: Interpretable Windows Expose Cross-Process Conflict and Pressure-Driven Rank Reversal
item.description: High-throughput screening identifies which metal–organic framework (MOF) ranks highest under one set of conditions, but not whether its design logic survives another operating regime. Here, we discover compact descriptor windows across 278,407 ARC-MOF ...

item.title: Single-GPU ML/MM Metadynamics Captures Enzyme-Bound Carbohydrate Puckering
item.description: Machine-learning/MM simulations promise QM/MM-like conformational sampling at molecular-dynamics cost, but practical proof-of-concept examples for enzyme-bound carbohydrates remain scarce. Here, we evaluate a MACE/AMBER well-tempered metadynamics protocol ...

item.title: Lewis Acid-Mediated Defluorination of Fluorinated Diluents in Battery Electrolytes
item.description: Fluorinated diluents have been widely adopted in lithium metal batteries in recent years and represent one of the most successful strategies for stabilizing the electrode-electrolyte interphase. Because these molecules interact only weakly with alkali ...

item.description: Synthetic mimics of biological condensates serve as a versatile platform for understanding the dynamics of liquid-liquid phase separation (LLPS) and the functional biomolecules encapsulated within the condensed microenvironment. In recent years, owing to ...

item.title: Single-and Multi-bead Coarse-grained Form Factors for SAXS Calculations
item.description: Small-angle X-ray scattering (SAXS) is widely used to study proteins in solution, but atomistic scattering calculations are computationally demanding for large systems and many-body simulations. We develop two coarse-grained, polynomial form factor models ...

item.description: Background. Ensemble docking can assign a high neural score to a ligand without showing whether the selected receptor-specific pose recovers interactions observed in target holo structures. We tested whether explicit recovery of target-native interactions ...

item.description: Two-dimensional (2D) materials beyond graphene have emerged as a diverse and rapidly expanding family of atomically thin structures with unique properties derived from quantum confinement, reduced dielectric screening, and high surface-to-volume ratios. ...

item.title: Electrosynthesis of free-standing asymmetric chitosan films at polarised soft interfaces
Iridium-Catalyzed Regio- and Enantioselective Hydroamination of Allenes: Direct Access to α-Tertiary Chiral Allylic Amines
item.description: We disclose a highly regio- and enantioselective Ir(I)-catalyzed hydroamination of 1,1-disubstituted allenes, providing a broadly applicable access to α-tertiary chiral allylic amines, very relevant structural motifs that are mostly inaccessible through ...

item.title: Self-Assembly Drives Rare-Earth Separations in a Family of M4L4 Cages
item.description: Rare-earth elements are indispensable to high-tech applications, making efficient rare-earth separations increasingly essential. An emerging approach to these challenging separations is the self-assembly of lanthanide-based supramolecular cages. In this ...

item.title: B, F Doped Graphitic Carbon Nitride (g-C3N4) as Efficient Volatile Organic Compound Sensing 2D Material: A DFT Study
item.description: 2D graphitic carbon nitride (g-C3N4) are achieving immense research interest due to their unique electrical, optical, and mechanical properties. In recent past, 2D nano materials have manifested their excellency for identifying disease-related volatile ...

item.title: N-Insertions on 2H-Azetines: A 4-to-5 Skeletal Editing Strategy towards Imidazoles
item.description: A nitrogen insertion ring-expansion strategy employing nitrene precursors has been developed for the conver-sion of 2H-azetines into the corresponding imidazoles. The transformation proceeds via transient nitrene spieces and involves C–C bond cleavage of ...

item.title: MOONLIT: A Trackable Antisense strategy targeting the miR-675-5p/HIF-1α axis in Glioblastoma
item.description: Glioblastoma remains one of the most aggressive and treatment-resistant brain tumours. Here, we demonstrate the therapeutic efficacy of a novel family of antisense oligonucleotides (MOONLITs) targeting the miR-675-5p/HIF-1α oncogenic axis. MOONLITs ...

item.title: High Chirality Induced Spin Selectivity in the Multilayered Moiré Superlattices of n-type as well as p-type 2D Crystalline Silicene Based on Resonant Quantum Tunneling
item.description: The recent efforts to fabricate spintronic devices based on layered two-dimensional (2D) van der Waals (vdW) materials through twisted stacking offers a promising avenue in electronics (especially spintronics) by taking advantage of quantum phenomena such ...

item.title: Determination of Asbestos Fibre Release Potential from Contaminated Craft Sand and Sand-Based Toys
item.description: Over the last century, human exposure pathways for asbestos have changed from those associated with direct interaction with the minerals (e.g. mining) to secondary exposure from a variety of asbestoscontaining materials, including those linked to para-...

item.title: Directional Electric Fields Reveal Nonadditive Polarization–Geometry Coupling in Cyclo[13]carbon Dichloride
item.description: Electronic polarization and nuclear relaxation can cooperate under oriented electric fields, but their nonadditivity is rarely resolved for deformable carbon rings. We computed the gas-phase, static, adiabatically relaxed response of singlet C13Cl2 in a ...

item.title: A Stretchable Interactive Photonic Electronic Hydrogel with Intrinsic Luminescent Timing Function for Strain Rate Recording
item.description: Stretchable electronics hold promising applications in diverse fields such as wearable healthcare devices and human-machine interfaces. However, current devices suffer from insufficient interactivity and reliance on external clock/timing modules. The ...

item.title: Hybrid Expert-Fuzzy Control for Dissolved Oxygen and Biomass Regulation in Extended-Aeration Activated Sludge Systems
item.description: Extended-aeration activated sludge systems are nonlinear and highly coupled biological processes whose performance depends on the coordinated regulation of dissolved oxygen, reactor biomass, returned biomass, sludge wasting, return sludge flow, and ...

item.title: Photoactivated chlorine dioxide oxygenation of freshly cleaved HOPG surfaces
Accurate Thermodynamics of Phenol Aggregates across Concentration and Temperature
item.description: Phenol aggregate populations in carbon tetrachloride are governed by an enthalpyentropy balance that varies with concentration and temperature. This redistribution is reflected in the infrared O H stretching band, whose position and shape provide a ...

item.title: Ball-Milling-Enabled Pd-Catalyzed One-Pot Cross-Coupling of Aryl Sulfonium Salts with In Situ Generated Reformatsky Enolates
item.description: Mechanochemistry enables an efficient Pd-catalyzed cross-coupling of aryl thianthrenium salts with in situ generated Reformatsky-type enolates. Ball milling plays a dual role by promoting both the activation of zinc for the formation of Reformatsky-type ...

item.title: Solvent-Controlled Formation of Arrested Polyelectrolyte–Surfactant Interfacial Assemblies and Their Impact on Emulsion Stability
item.description: Arrested interfaces formed through confined polyelectrolyte–surfactant association offer a promising route for designing emulsion systems with tailored properties. However, the impact of organic solvents on the formation, organization, and functionality ...

item.title: Phase-Aware Adaptation for Functional Group Recognition fromCondensed-Phase Infrared Spectra
item.description: Infrared (IR) spectroscopy is widely used for functional group identification, but machine-learning models often struggle when spectra are transferred across physical phases. Here we establish a phase-separated 16-label benchmark for functional group ...

item.title: Excited-State Intramolecular Proton Transfer Dynamics with Neural Network Potentials and Nuclear Quantum Effects
item.description: Excited-state intramolecular proton transfer is a prototypical ultrafast process that forms the foundation for many chemical and biological phenomena and is highly sensitive to changes in the underlying potential energy landscape. Here, we develop neural ...

item.title: Fluorescence-based post-mortem interval estimation using cell lysate from DNA/RNA co-extraction of human soft tissue
item.description: Post-mortem interval (PMI) estimation is an important element in medico-legal death investigations, as reliable estimates can establish timelines and generate investigative leads. Various analytical techniques are used to monitor biomolecular degradation ...

item.title: Dissecting Dual Ir/Lewis base Mechanophotoredox Catalysis by Ex Situ Kinetic and Thermographic Profiling for Solvent-free Air-tolerant Giese-type Addition of Boronic esters onto Electron-Deficient Olefins
item.description: We describe a dual Lewis base mechanophotocatalytic protocol for Giese-type additions that unites an iridium photocatalyst with Lewis bases under solvent-minimized or solvent-free conditions. The combination of mechanical agitation and visible-light ...

item.title: One-pot systhesis of a long-chain metal-backboned polymer
item.description: Metal-backboned polymers, featuring backbones composed exclusively of metal atoms interconnected by consecutive metal–metal bonds, have been proposed as a new class of materials for promising applications in optics, electronics, and magnetics; however, ...

item.title: Metal-and Additive-Free Transfer Hydrogenation of Nitrones to Hydroxylamine Derivatives with PhSiH3
item.description: Hydroxylamine derivatives are essential structural motifs in numerous pharmaceuticals, bioactive compounds, and synthetic intermediates. Despite significant advances, the reduction of nitrones remains challenging owing to their inherent 1,3-dipolar ...

item.title: Rapid, energy-lean sonochemical synthesis of metal-free polybenzoxazine bifunctional photocatalyst for H2O2 production and furfuryl alcohol oxidation
item.description: The development of scalable, selective, and durable photocatalysts remains a major challenge in artificial photosynthesis. Herein, we introduce a new class of metal-free polybenzoxazine frameworks synthesized through an unconventional template- and ...

item.title: Breaking Enantiopurity Paradigm: A Chiral Catalyst That Requires Enantioimpurity for Enantioselectivity
item.description: Asymmetric catalysis traditionally relies on enantiopure ligands to achieve high enantioselectivity. However, nonlinear effects (NLEs) can enable scalemic catalysts to match or even surpass their enantiopure counterparts. Here, we report an unprecedented ...

item.title: Linking wind-power fluctuation to degradation of PEM water electrolyzer
item.description: Proton exchange membrane (PEM) water electrolysis is highly attractive for renewable-powered hydrogen production, yet its degradation under realistic wind-power fluctuations remains insufficiently understood. In this study, a durability assessment ...

item.title: Ageing mechanisms and regeneration of an LMFP/NMC blended cathode
item.description: Ageing and recycling studies are essential to assess the durability and circularity of emerging commercial battery chemistries. Here, the ageing mechanisms of a commercial lithium-ion cells containing an LMFP/NMC blended cathode are investigated after ...

item.title: Smolyak Hierarchy-Inspired Protocol (SHIP): Hierarchical Exploration of Sparse-Reward Spaces with GFlowNets
item.description: Generative modelling is a useful methodology for large-scale combinatorial problems involving many possible candidates. Such problems typically suffer from exponential scaling in complexity as the size of the system being studied is increased, and this ...

item.title: Phosphoestamer Sequence Controls Capture/Release
item.description: While the synthesis of sequence-defined polymers is becoming established, the next frontier is sequence-determined function. Sequenced-defined oligo/polyphosphoesters (phosphoestamers) of dodecane diol (C12) and hexa(ethylene glycol) (HEG) show sequence-...

item.title: Harnessing phosphine oxides interactions with HF and HBF4 to enable the hydrofluorination of alkynes
item.description: Synthetic methodologies for the metal-free fluorofunctionalisation of alkynes to give hydrofluorination products still remain scarce. In this paper, we report on detailed studies on the interaction between the phosphine oxides R3P=O (R = Cy, Ph, Morph) ...

item.title: Towards unified catalyst discovery through transferable composition–activity relationships
item.description: Multinary materials provide vast compositional flexibility for optimizing electrocatalytic reactions, yet systematic exploration of their composition space is challenging. Density functional theory (DFT)-derived models, thin-film materials libraries for ...

item.title: Expanding Metal Complex Enzyme Inhibitor Discovery Through High-Throughput Virtual Screening
item.description: Transition metal complexes occupy regions of three-dimensional chemical space that organic scaffolds cannot easily reach, yet their use in target-based drug discovery has been held back by the absence of structurally diverse, readily available screening ...

item.title: Post mortem insights into swollen LiFePO4 batteries: reversible degradation and suitability for direct recycling
item.description: Lithium iron phosphate (LFP) batteries are increasingly deployed in electric vehicles, raising concerns regarding the management of prematurely retired cells, particularly those affected by swelling. In this work, a comprehensive post mortem investigation ...

item.title: One pot synthesis of carbon dots derived from d-glucose monohydrate: Comprehensive investigation on optical properties and luminescent mechanism
item.description: The best optical properties of N-CDs from D-glucose monohydrate and urea have been synthesized. The use of D-glucose monohydrate as a carbon source and urea as a nitrogen source offers many benefits, including low cost, easy availability, high quantum ...

item.title: Photoluminescent and photoconductive BaZrS3 films grown at device-compatible temperatures from stable sulfide precursors
item.description: Chalcogenide perovskites (CPs) are attractive earth-abundant, non-toxic light absorbers. BaZrS3 is a prominent candidate in the CP family due to its 1.7-2.0 eV band gap, which aligns well with a top absorber in Si-based tandem solar cells. The main ...

item.title: Ultrasensitive Polymeric Fluorescent Nanothermometers Based on Environment-Sensitive DCM-Like Dyes: Decoupling Aggregation, Polarity, and Viscosity Contributions
item.description: Temperature measurements at the nanoscale are essential for understanding physiological and pathological processes. Here, we report fluorescent polymeric nanothermometers (FPTSs) based on thermoresponsive poly(N-isopropylacrylamide) (PNIPAM) ...

item.title: Assembly-by-Elimination of 3D Nanoarchitectures with Designed Interstitials and Interweaves
item.description: The realization of metamaterials with tailored functional responses requires three-dimensional (3D) architectures that combine low packing fractions with complex topology. Such metastructures can enhance strength-to-weight ratios, accommodate large ...

item.title: Tuning hydrogel mechanics through synergistic metal–ligand coordination and chemical fueling
item.description: Chemically fueled anhydride formation offers a powerful strategy to create polymer networks with transient mechanical reinforcement. In this work, metallo-supramolecular hydrogels were developed with two independent crosslinking mechanisms, metal-ligand ...

item.title: Programming Rotational Register to Prescribe Crystal Polymorphism
item.description: Crystal polymorphism underlies many of the distinct properties of condensed matter, yet for atoms and molecules, polymorphs are typically selected by thermodynamics and kinetics rather than by design. Programming crystal polymorphism is particularly ...

item.title: Experimental and Simulation Study of Foliar Nanofertilizers through Cellulose-nanofibril Templating
item.description: Despite the higher absorption efficiency of foliar fertilizers compared to soil fertilizers, the risk of fertilizer loss remains. Additionally, traditional foliar fertilizers tend to form large, irregular particles on leaves, hindering plant absorption. ...

item.title: Mechanistic Determinants of Passive Permeation in Binary Lipid Membranes from Molecular Dynamics Simulations
item.description: Passive permeation is a fundamental determinant of pharmacokinetics. The models that simulate permeation pathways and predict their energetic requirements need to account for the complexity of biological membranes. However, the impact of lipid bilayer ...

item.title: Fragment-based discovery of mycobacterial thioredoxin reductase inhibitors guided by virtual chemical space exploration
item.description: The rising number of people dying from tuberculosis and the increase of drug-resistant Mycobacterium tuberculosis (Mtb) strains lead to an urgent need for new antituberculosis drugs. The mycobacterial thioredoxin reductase (Mtb-TrxR) is a novel and ...

item.title: Beyond Global Drug-Likeness: The FamDLike Framework as a Target Family-specific Druglikeness Score
item.description: Drug-likeness is a central metric which is used to evaluate the physicochemical profile of small molecules either as a filter for virtual screening or, more recently, as a reward function in generative design tasks. Established scores such as ED are ...

item.title: 3D Printing of Metal–Organic Polyhedra: From Porous Molecules to Spatially Programmed Porous Architectures
item.description: Additive manufacturing of microporous materials remains challenging due to the intrinsic processing limitations of extended porous frameworks, which often require binders or complex inks. Herein, we report the first 3D printing of metal–organic polyhedra (...

item.title: Foldamers with Two Distinct Helical Motifs Controlled by Solvent
item.description: Abiotic foldamers that switch between distinct, well-defined geometries are rare. Here, we report a structurally simple series of oligomers with alternatingortho-phenylene and 2,3-pyrazinylene repeat units. Deconvolution of variable-temperature ¹H NMR ...

item.title: Heptacoordinated Antimony(V) Cations
item.description: Heptacoordinated pnictogen cations are quite rare, although cationic Sb(V) species are formally isoelectronic with neutral Sn(IV) compounds, for which several heptacoordinated structures are known. Here, we report on the synthesis, isolation, and ...

item.title: Semicrystalline Chain-growth Photopolymer Networks Synthesized by Orthogonal Neat Polymerization Reactions
item.description: A sequential and orthogonal polymerization reaction strategy was developed to synthesize semicrystalline polymer networks. Acrylate-terminated oligomers were first prepared by thiol-Michael oligomerization between difunctional aliphatic acrylate and thiol ...

item.title: PDB Manipulation Suite (PDBMS): A browser-based toolkit for PDB file manipulation, molecule building and biophysical analysis, easily extendable via reasoning models
item.description: We present here the PDB Manipulation Suite (PDBMS,https://pdbms.altervista.org/), a modular hub of web apps and platforms designed for the seamless creation, edition, and analysis of biomolecular coordinate files, all online and client-side i.e. without ...

item.title: Native multi-dimensional (SEC/CZE/SID) protein complex identification and topology characterization (Native MudPIT)
item.description: The initiation and progression of diseases are usually due to biological processes going awry, driven by proteins in complexes that often assemble via noncovalent interactions of monomeric or multimeric subunits. A broader view of protein complex dynamics ...

item.title: An Alternative Strategy for Albumin Binding in Therapeutics. Proof-of-Concept Synthesis of Metallacarborane–Insulin Conjugates.
item.description: Fear of strict dosing schedules and associated hypoglycaemic events often leads to significant delays in the initiation of insulin therapy in diabetic patients who do not achieve glycaemic targets with oral drugs. To better address these concerns new long-...

item.title: Highly stable microporous water for energy absorption
item.description: Water has poor compressibility. However, introducing hydrophobic porosity into water can make it highly compressible, as water molecules can be squeezed into the hydrophobic pores under pressure. This offers an exciting opportunity to use water to absorb ...

item.title: Non-Significant Effect of Water Content and Precursor Concentration on the Synthesis of Monodisperse SiO2@FexOy Colloidal Spheres
item.description: Monodisperse spherical colloidal particles with core–shell structures, such as SiO₂@shell, are the focus of extensive research. However, those coated with iron oxide (SiO2@FexOy) have received comparatively little attention, rendering them a promising ...
